title: Stability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products
description: Provides guidance on the design of stability testing studies for new drug substances and drug products, including conditions, time points, and acceptance criteria. Covers long-term, intermediate, and accelerated storage conditions for climatic zones.

title: Photostability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products
description: Provides guidance on photostability testing as an integral part of the stress testing strategy for new drug substances and products. Defines standard light exposure conditions and sample handling procedures.

title: Stability Testing for New Dosage Forms
description: Addresses stability testing requirements for new dosage forms of already-approved drug substances. Outlines reduced testing protocols when prior stability data from the original dosage form is available.

title: Bracketing and Matrixing Designs for Stability Testing of New Drug Substances and Products
description: Describes reduced stability testing designs using bracketing and matrixing approaches. Provides guidance on when and how these statistical approaches may be applied to reduce the number of samples tested.

title: Evaluation of Stability Data
description: Provides guidance on the evaluation of stability data to propose retest periods or shelf lives for drug substances and products. Describes statistical methods for analyzing stability data including pooling and extrapolation approaches.

title: Validation of Analytical Procedures
description: Provides a comprehensive framework for the validation of analytical procedures used in pharmaceutical development and manufacturing. The revised version integrates analytical procedure development concepts and addresses modern analytical technologies including multivariate methods.

title: Impurities in New Drug Substances
description: Provides guidance on the classification, identification, and qualification of impurities in new drug substances produced by chemical synthesis. Establishes reporting, identification, and qualification thresholds based on daily dose.

title: Impurities in New Drug Products
description: Provides guidance on the classification, identification, and qualification of degradation products in new drug products. Outlines reporting and qualification thresholds for impurities arising during manufacturing or storage.

title: Residual Solvents
description: Recommends acceptable amounts for residual solvents in pharmaceuticals based on toxicity data. Classifies solvents into three categories based on risk assessment and provides permitted daily exposure limits.

title: Guideline for Elemental Impurities
description: Provides a comprehensive approach to limiting elemental impurities in pharmaceutical products. Establishes permitted daily exposure values for elemental impurities and a risk-based approach for their control.

title: Quality of Biotechnological Products: Viral Safety Evaluation of Biotechnology Products Derived from Cell Lines of Human or Animal Origin
description: Provides guidance on the strategy for viral safety evaluation of biotechnology-derived products from cell lines. The revised version modernizes approaches to virus testing, clearance studies, and emerging risk considerations for novel modalities.

title: Quality of Biotechnological Products: Stability Testing of Biotechnological/Biological Products
description: Provides specific guidance on the stability testing of biotechnological and biological products, addressing unique characteristics such as protein degradation, aggregation, and potency loss. Supplements Q1A with considerations specific to biological macromolecules.

title: Comparability of Biotechnological/Biological Products Subject to Changes in Their Manufacturing Process
description: Provides principles for assessing the comparability of biotechnological/biological products before and after manufacturing process changes. Describes a stepwise approach using analytical, nonclinical, and clinical comparability studies.

title: Specifications: Test Procedures and Acceptance Criteria for New Drug Substances and New Drug Products: Chemical Substances
description: Provides guidance on setting specifications (tests, procedures, and acceptance criteria) for new drug substances and drug products of chemical origin. Covers universal and specific tests for identity, assay, impurities, and physical attributes.

title: Specifications: Test Procedures and Acceptance Criteria for Biotechnological/Biological Products
description: Addresses setting specifications for biotechnological and biological products, including considerations for characterization, purity, potency, and quantity. Covers protein-specific analytical methods and reference standards.

title: Good Manufacturing Practice Guide for Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients
description: Provides guidance on good manufacturing practice (GMP) for the manufacture of active pharmaceutical ingredients (APIs). Covers quality management, personnel, buildings, process equipment, documentation, and materials management for API production.

title: Pharmaceutical Development
description: Provides guidance on the contents of the pharmaceutical development section of a regulatory submission. Promotes a systematic, science-based approach to development including Quality by Design (QbD) principles and design space concepts.

title: Quality Risk Management
description: Provides a systematic approach to quality risk management throughout the pharmaceutical product lifecycle. The revised version adds emphasis on formality of risk management activities, subjectivity mitigation, and risk-based decision making in GMP.

title: Pharmaceutical Quality System
description: Describes a model for an effective pharmaceutical quality system based on ISO quality concepts and GMP regulations. Covers management responsibilities, continual improvement, knowledge management, and process performance monitoring.

title: Development and Manufacture of Drug Substances (Chemical Entities and Biotechnological/Biological Entities)
description: Provides guidance on development and manufacturing information for drug substances, covering both chemical and biotechnological/biological entities. Describes approaches for establishing design spaces and applying enhanced process understanding to manufacturing.

title: Technical and Regulatory Considerations for Pharmaceutical Product Lifecycle Management
description: Provides a framework to facilitate the management of post-approval chemistry, manufacturing, and controls (CMC) changes. Introduces concepts of established conditions and reporting categories to enable innovation and continual improvement.

title: Continuous Manufacturing of Drug Substances and Drug Products
description: Provides scientific and regulatory considerations specific to continuous manufacturing of drug substances and drug products. Addresses batch definition, control strategy, process monitoring, real-time release testing, and process validation for continuous processes.

title: Analytical Procedure Development
description: Provides principles and scientific approaches for analytical procedure development to encourage a more enhanced and systematic development approach. Covers minimal and enhanced approaches, analytical target profiles, and multivariate analytical procedures.

title: Need for Carcinogenicity Studies of Pharmaceuticals
description: Defines the conditions under which carcinogenicity studies should be conducted for pharmaceuticals. Outlines criteria based on expected clinical use duration, patient population, and prior signals of concern.

title: Testing for Carcinogenicity of Pharmaceuticals
description: Provides updated guidance on approaches for carcinogenicity testing using a weight-of-evidence evaluation to determine the need for studies. The revised version introduces a risk-based approach that may reduce the use of 2-year rat bioassays in favor of integrated hazard assessments.

title: Guidance on Genotoxicity Testing and Data Interpretation for Pharmaceuticals Intended for Human Use
description: Provides a standard battery of genotoxicity tests and guidance on interpreting results for human pharmaceuticals. Covers in vitro and in vivo testing strategies, follow-up approaches, and evaluation of positive results in the context of overall risk assessment.

title: Note for Guidance on Toxicokinetics: The Assessment of Systemic Exposure in Toxicity Studies
description: Provides guidance on generating pharmacokinetic data within toxicology studies to aid interpretation of toxicity findings. Describes the integration of toxicokinetic assessments into the design of nonclinical toxicity studies to relate systemic exposure to dose and toxicological endpoints.

title: Detection of Reproductive and Developmental Toxicity for Human Pharmaceuticals
description: Provides updated guidance on the detection of reproductive and developmental toxicity of human pharmaceuticals. The revision introduces flexibility in study designs and incorporates approaches to reduce animal use while maintaining sensitivity for hazard detection.

title: Preclinical Safety Evaluation of Biotechnology-Derived Pharmaceuticals
description: Outlines a science-based approach for preclinical safety evaluation of biotechnology-derived pharmaceuticals including proteins and peptides. Addresses species selection, study design, immunogenicity assessment, and reproductive toxicity considerations specific to biotherapeutics.

title: Safety Pharmacology Studies for Human Pharmaceuticals
description: Defines the objectives, strategies, and principles for safety pharmacology studies of human pharmaceuticals. Describes a core battery of studies evaluating effects on cardiovascular, central nervous, and respiratory systems.

title: Nonclinical Evaluation of the Potential for Delayed Ventricular Repolarization (QT Interval Prolongation) by Human Pharmaceuticals
description: Provides guidance on nonclinical strategies to evaluate the potential of drug candidates to delay ventricular repolarization. The revised version integrates newer in silico and in vitro approaches for more comprehensive proarrhythmic risk assessment.

title: Immunotoxicity Studies for Human Pharmaceuticals
description: Provides guidance on nonclinical immunotoxicity testing strategies for human pharmaceuticals. Outlines a weight-of-evidence approach for determining whether additional immunotoxicity studies are needed based on findings from standard toxicity studies.

title: Nonclinical Evaluation for Anticancer Pharmaceuticals
description: Provides guidance on the nonclinical safety evaluation of anticancer pharmaceuticals, recognizing the unique risk-benefit considerations for this patient population. Addresses reduced nonclinical requirements to avoid unnecessary delays in patient access to oncology treatments.

title: Photosafety Evaluation of Pharmaceuticals
description: Provides a tiered approach for photosafety evaluation of pharmaceuticals, starting with an assessment of photoreactive potential. Describes in vitro and in vivo photosafety testing strategies and clinical assessment approaches.

title: Nonclinical Safety Testing in Support of Development of Paediatric Medicines
description: Provides guidance on when juvenile animal studies should be conducted to support paediatric drug development. Addresses study design considerations, species selection, and timing relative to clinical paediatric trials.

title: Nonclinical Biodistribution Considerations for Gene Therapy Products
description: Provides recommendations on nonclinical biodistribution studies for gene therapy products to support clinical development. Addresses study design, analytical methodology, and integration of biodistribution data with other nonclinical and clinical findings.

title: Clinical Safety Data Management: Definitions and Standards for Expedited Reporting
description: Establishes standard definitions and terminology for clinical safety data reporting, including adverse events, adverse drug reactions, and unexpected adverse reactions. Defines criteria and timelines for expedited reporting of serious adverse events during clinical development.

title: Good Clinical Practice
description: Provides an international ethical and scientific quality standard for the design, conduct, recording, and reporting of clinical trials. Serves as the foundational guideline for protecting trial participant rights and ensuring data integrity and credibility.

title: General Considerations for Clinical Studies
description: Provides updated guidance on the general considerations for planning and designing clinical studies throughout the drug development lifecycle. Emphasizes a quality-by-design approach to clinical study design, fit-for-purpose data collection, and stakeholder engagement.

title: Statistical Principles for Clinical Trials
description: Provides statistical principles for clinical trials with an addendum addressing estimands and sensitivity analyses. The R1 addendum introduces a structured framework for defining treatment effects through the estimands concept, aligning trial objectives with statistical analysis.

title: The Clinical Evaluation of QT/QTc Interval Prolongation and Proarrhythmic Potential for Non-Antiarrhythmic Drugs
description: Describes clinical strategies for evaluating the potential of non-antiarrhythmic drugs to delay cardiac repolarization, as detected by QT/QTc prolongation. Defines the thorough QT study and criteria for evaluating QTc results in regulatory submissions.

title: General Principles for Planning and Design of Multi-Regional Clinical Trials
description: Provides strategic guidance for the planning and design of multi-regional clinical trials (MRCTs) that are acceptable to regulatory authorities across multiple regions. Addresses sample size considerations, regional consistency evaluation, and the use of MRCTs for simultaneous global drug development.

title: Optimisation of Safety Data Collection
description: Provides recommendations to optimize the collection of safety data in clinical trials by focusing on the most relevant information for safety assessment. Aims to reduce unnecessary data collection burden while maintaining rigorous safety monitoring.

title: Adaptive Clinical Trials
description: Provides guidance on the design and conduct of adaptive clinical trials that allow prospectively planned modifications based on accumulating data. Covers types of adaptations, statistical considerations, operational aspects, and regulatory reporting requirements.

title: Medical Dictionary for Regulatory Activities (MedDRA)
description: Provides the framework for MedDRA, the internationally standardized medical terminology used for regulatory communication and evaluation of data related to medicinal products. Covers terminology structure, points to consider for use, and term selection guidance.

title: Electronic Standards for the Transfer of Regulatory Information (ESTRI)
description: Establishes electronic standards for the electronic transmission of regulatory information between pharmaceutical industry and regulatory authorities. Defines messaging standards, electronic gateway specifications, and data exchange formats.

title: Nonclinical Safety Studies for the Conduct of Human Clinical Trials and Marketing Authorization for Pharmaceuticals
description: Provides recommendations on the nonclinical safety studies needed to support clinical trial phases and marketing authorization. Defines the timing and nature of required toxicology, pharmacology, and pharmacokinetic studies relative to clinical development milestones.

title: Organisation of the Common Technical Document for the Registration of Pharmaceuticals for Human Use
description: Defines the harmonized format and structure of the Common Technical Document (CTD) for pharmaceutical regulatory submissions across ICH regions. Covers the organization of quality, safety, and efficacy documentation into a five-module structure used globally.

title: Assessment and Control of DNA Reactive (Mutagenic) Impurities in Pharmaceuticals to Limit Potential Carcinogenic Risk
description: Provides a framework for the identification, assessment, and control of mutagenic impurities in pharmaceutical products. Outlines acceptable intake limits, structure-activity relationship evaluation, and control strategies including the Threshold of Toxicological Concern (TTC) approach.

title: Bioanalytical Method Validation and Study Sample Analysis
description: Provides comprehensive guidance on the validation of bioanalytical methods and the analysis of study samples for pharmaceutical development. Covers chromatographic and ligand binding assays with specific validation parameters, acceptance criteria, and documentation requirements.

title: Bioequivalence for Immediate-Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms
description: Provides harmonized guidance on the design and conduct of bioequivalence studies for immediate-release solid oral dosage forms. Addresses study design, statistical methods, acceptance criteria, and the use of the Biopharmaceutics Classification System (BCS) for biowaiver applications.
